title: Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act, 2027
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 605.
summary: The Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act, 2027, is a US law that allocates funds for the Department of Homeland Security. This act aims to fund various programs and services within the department to improve homeland security.

title: Departments of Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2027
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 604.
summary: The Departments of Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2027 is a proposed policy framework outlining funding allocations for various government departments in 2027. The policy aims to allocate federal funds for labor, health and human services, and education-related programs and initiatives.

title: Condemning actors seeking to defraud the United States Government, and expressing the sense of the House of Representatives that governmentwide fraud and improper payment prevention reforms will meaningfully improve the financial prosperity of the United States, and that Federal program eligibility should be verified before payment.
action: Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
summary: The US House of Representatives is condemning fraud against the government and calling for better ways to prevent it. The goal is to improve financial prosperity by verifying eligibility before making payments through government programs.

title: Allowing Emancipation Hall to be used for a ceremony to dedicate the Semiquincentennial Congressional Time Capsule on Wednesday, June 24, 2026.
action: Received in the Senate.
summary: This policy allows Emancipation Hall to be used on June 24, 2026, for a ceremony related to the dedication of the Semiquincentennial Congressional Time Capsule. The event is scheduled to take place on a specific date.

title: PATH Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.
summary: The PATH Act provides guidelines for the operation and development of artificial intelligence systems. Unfortunately, with limited information, the specifics of this document's implementation and regulations are unknown.

title: Alice Cogswell and Anne Sullivan Macy Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: The Alice Cogswell and Anne Sullivan Macy Act is a US policy aimed at improving access to communication services for individuals who are blind or have low vision.

title: Increasing Insurance Access for Specialty Crops Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.
summary: The Increasing Insurance Access for Specialty Crops Act aims to expand insurance options for farmers growing specialty crops, providing them with greater financial security. This legislation likely addresses the specific needs of farmers who grow unique and often higher-risk crops to help them better manage risks and increase their access to insurance coverage.

title: Improving Pest and Disease Preparedness for Specialty Crops Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.
summary: This document aims to improve preparedness for pest and disease threats in specialty crops to protect their health and stability. It likely outlines measures or a plan to enhance pest and disease management in the specialty crop sector.

title: PEACE Act

Based on the title alone, it's possible that PEACE Act stands for "Providing for Equitable Access to Cybersecurity Credit in History Education Act" but exact details require more information.

title: Federal Fraud Prevention Workforce Training Act
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: The Federal Fraud Prevention Workforce Training Act is a law that aims to establish training programs for government employees to prevent and detect fraud within the federal workforce. The goal of this act is to help prevent financial losses and protect American taxpayers from fraudulent activities within the federal government.

title: Mandatory E-Verify Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Mandatory E-Verify Act of 2026 is a policy that requires businesses to use a government-run system to verify the immigration status of their employees. This policy aims to improve immigration enforcement and ensure that all workers in the US are authorized to work.
